codex_response:
Implemented task `29700607` in [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_29700607/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_29700607/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_29700607/generator.py#L1).

Rule summary: each top-row color grows a vertical segment downward; if that color has a second singleton elsewhere, the segment turns horizontally on that row to reach it, otherwise it continues to the bottom edge. I used the Opus hint as the starting hypothesis because it matches the official examples; the Sonnet entry was also consistent enough, so no [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_29700607/notes.md) was needed. No new primitive was added.

The generator stays close to the observed family: square grids in the official size range, a centered 3-color anchor on row 0, border singleton endpoints, and only the `LLR`, `LRR`, or missing-middle layouts seen in the official examples. Verification passed on `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples.
